mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and lowers the opportunity cost of holding stocks relative to cash, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.1 at snapshot indicating zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts negative average forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 8%, indicating an acute labor market shock, equity markets price in near-term earnings deterioration and consumption weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as the shock is digested.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.0 at snapshot exceeding the 8% threshold
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where UNRATE > 8%, the subsequent 5-day SPX return is positive, or the mean forward return during triggered periods is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) signals a broad-based contraction in consumer and residential demand that leads corporate revenue guidance lower over the following quarter, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. Neither series alone captures the demand-side deterioration; both must weaken together.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=549.0 reflecting depressed housing starts at snapshot
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST decline episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction term contributes no incremental Rank IC over either single-series model in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and supply-side output growth, which forward-signals improving corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend and forward 60d returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns are negative in more than half of periods with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero while CPIAUCSL simultaneously accelerates, markets anticipate a policy dilemma between supporting growth and containing inflation, elevating risk premia and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. This effect requires both the zero-rate constraint and rising inflation jointly; neither alone triggers the mechanism.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.1 at snapshot near zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during joint zero-rate/rising-inflation episodes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series FEDFUNDS or CPIAUCSL models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in PAYEMS following a prior labor market collapse (3-month change exceeding 500,000 persons) signals the onset of a post-shock recovery, leading investors to price in resumed consumption and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=131028.0 at snapshot reflecting depressed employment level post-shock
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical episodes meeting the PAYEMS rebound trigger, forward 20-day SPX returns are negative, or the signal's average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) versus unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 (real GDP) reflects durable aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as long as the growth trend persists across at least two consecutive releases.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth trend and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the sign hit-rate drops below 50% out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below 600 thousand units signals acute weakness in residential investment, a leading indicator of broader economic distress, causing risk-averse repricing of equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=549.0 at snapshot below the 600k threshold
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where HOUST < 600k, or the average triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).
